what_it_does: Documents a proposed diff to change the empty-corpus note returned by query_index in store/vector_index.py so it no longer blames the retired :8001 embedder or suggests fallback, and instead explains that durable records live in named spaces and suggests passing a space parameter.
observations: It is a diff note/proposal, not an applied patch
The OLD note mentions the retired :8001 embedder and tells the caller to fall back
The NEW note distinguishes the default/unspaced index from named spaces: history, common_knowledge, principles, worldview, topics, repo
It provides a conditional hint: one message when space is None and a different one when a specific space is empty
It cites recollection channel ch-83e2cque as the source of the proposal
It includes exact target file path and line numbers (store/vector_index.py:170-174)
No code symbols are declared in the file
standouts: Targets a specific UX trap: a misleading empty-default-space message
Reorients the caller from generic fallback to selecting a named memory space
Removes the retired :8001 reference and only mentions :8007 in the already-spaced branch
concerns: The file is a proposal, so it is unclear whether the diff has been applied or tested
It hard-codes a list of space names that could become stale
It references specific service ports (:8001, :8007) that may drift
It assumes the default index is almost empty, which may not hold in all deployments
notes: A proposed diff note that rewords the empty vector-index response to point callers at named memory spaces instead of a retired embedder.
questions: Has this diff been applied and tested in store/vector_index.py?
What are the exact definitions of _scope and emb in the surrounding code?
How are the listed space names maintained and validated?
How authoritative is the recollection from ch-83e2cque?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to fix a misleading empty-default-space note, and the file does exactly that by supplying an OLD/NEW diff plus rationale, so it matches.
apparent_themes: Named memory spaces versus the default index
intention_signals: It tries to stop callers from interpreting an empty default vector index as missing data or a broken embedder, and instead steer them to query the appropriate named space.
novelty: It converts a generic empty-index message into a contextual guide that names specific semantic spaces and gives a concrete usage example.
contribution: It contributes a precise user-facing wording change and the reasoning behind it, which a fused system could keep as guidance for how empty vector-index responses should direct callers toward named spaces.
summary_for_embedding: A proposed diff for store/vector_index.py that replaces a misleading empty-index note blaming a retired embedder with a message directing callers to query named memory spaces such as history, common_knowledge, and repo.
intention_for_embedding: To fix the UX trap where an empty default vector index leads users to believe records are missing or to fall back, by explaining that durable records reside in named spaces.
